case.focal_claim: ... [the study] finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs [compared to villages dominated by upper caste].

execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python 3.10 Docker image and executed the two planned mixed-effects models using the original Stata datasets. Fixed missing dependencies and a minor script syntax error. Both tasks completed and produced outputs. The key ST/SC-by-domhigh interaction was positive and not significant, providing no support for the focal claim; one other low-caste interaction (Back oth) was positive and significant, opposite to the claim.

deviations.description: Added scipy, patsy, pyreadstat, and statsmodels to analysis_info.json; embedded a runtime pip installer into the Python scripts to ensure libraries are available in the container.
deviations.reason: Container execution initially failed due to missing statsmodels and mismatched environments.

deviations.description: Fixed a duplicated import that caused a syntax error after adding the runtime installer code.
deviations.reason: A small edit introduced a duplicated import line which broke Python parsing.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: The planned mixed-effects model and data path were followed exactly; only dependency and minor syntax fixes were made.

test_statistics.other: Coefficient corresponds to C(caste)[T.ST/SC]:domhigh from MixedLM with random intercept for village; domhigh=1 is high-caste dominated.

result_raw.result_text: For ST/SC households, the estimated difference between high-caste dominated vs low-caste dominated villages was +2078 income units (z=1.32, p=0.186), not statistically significant.

conclusion.conclusion_text: Results do not support the claim; the ST/SC-by-domhigh effect is positive (not negative) and not significant. For Back oth, the interaction is positive and significant, which is opposite to the claim.

deviations.description: Embedded a runtime pip installer to ensure statsmodels/patsy availability consistent with container permissions.
deviations.reason: Initial run environment lacked required Python packages.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Same planned MixedLM executed with no prohibited controls added, matching the restrictions.

test_statistics.other: Z-values reported for fixed effects; same MixedLM as Task1. Coefficient is C(caste)[T.ST/SC]:domhigh.

result_raw.result_text: For ST/SC, the domhigh contrast (high vs low caste domination) is +2078 income units (z=1.32, p=0.186).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Again, results do not support the claim. The direction is opposite (positive), and not statistically significant.
